2604.24395.title: Aligning with Your Own Voice: Self-Corrected Preference Learning for Hallucination Mitigation in LVLMs
2604.24395.paper_summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) frequently suffer from hallucinations. Existing preference learning-based approaches largely rely on proprietary models to construct preference datasets. We identify that this reliance introduces a distributional mismatch between the proprietary and target models that hinders efficient alignment. To address this, we propose Alignment via VErified Self-correction DPO (AVES-DPO), a framework that aligns LVLMs using in-distribution data derived from the model's intrinsic knowledge. Our approach employs a consensus-based verification mechanism to diagnose diverse hallucinations and guides the model to self-correct, thereby generating preference pairs strictly compatible with its internal distribution. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AVES-DPO surpasses existing baselines in hallucination mitigation while requiring only 5.2k samples.
2604.24395.paper_summary_zh: 大型視覺語言模型（LVLMs）經常遭遇幻覺問題。現有的基於偏好學習的方法主要依賴專有模型來構建偏好數據集。我們發現這種依賴引入了專有模型與目標模型之間的分佈不匹配，這妨礙了有效的對齊。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了通過經過驗證的自我修正DPO（AVES-DPO）的對齊框架，該框架利用從模型內在知識中派生的內部數據來對齊LVLMs。我們的方法採用基於共識的驗證機制來診斷多樣的幻覺，並指導模型自我修正，從而生成與其內部分佈嚴格兼容的偏好對。大量實驗表明，AVES-DPO在減輕幻覺方面超越了現有基準，同時僅需5.2k樣本。
2604.24395.author: Byeonggeuk Lim et.al.
2604.24395.authors: Byeonggeuk Lim, JungMin Yun, Junehyoung Kwon, Kyeonghyun Kim, YoungBin Kim
